statement.p:
Consider the graph of the equation y={\left(x-\frac{7}{6}\right)^{2}-\frac{5}{7}}\text{.} Compared to the graph of y=x^2\text{,} the vertex has been shifted units
?
left
right and units
?
down
up .

solution.p: Since the vertex is \left({{\frac{7}{6}}},{-{\frac{5}{7}}}\right)\text{,} the graph is the graph of y=x^2 shifted (7/6) units right and (5/7) units down.
